L1.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists in the certificate authority's token validation logic. When certain provisioner types do not support token-based authentication flows, the token reuse protection mechanism silently fails open, allowing an attacker to bypass authorization checks entirely. Additionally, revocation endpoints do not verify that the serial number in the request matches the serial number in the presented credential, enabling unauthorized certificate revocation.

ground_truth.reason: The token reuse validation in the authorization path silently ignores errors from provisioners that do not support token flows (ACME and SCEP), completely bypassing authorization. Additionally, revocation endpoints fail to verify serial number consistency between requests and credentials.
